items.title: Breeding male mole-rats (Fukomys) use strong bites to defend reproductive monopoly
items.author: Matěj Lövy
items.content: Breeding status shapes bite force in social African Fukomys mole-rats: strong male competition drives higher bite force in breeding males, while weaker female competition results in lower bite force in breeding females.

items.title: Morph-specific selection drives phenotypic divergence in color polymorphic tawny owls (Strix aluco) in Northern Europe
items.author: Patrik Karell
items.content: Long-term study reveals how intra-morph plumage color variation in a polymorphic bird shapes morph-specific evolutionary responses to environmental change, highlighting complex selection dynamics acting within genetically discrete color morphs.

items.title: MDM2-P53-V-ATPases axis driven by dehydroevodiamine to fight intracellular bacterial infection
items.author: Jianfeng Wang
items.content: Lysosomal activation via the MDM2-P53-V-ATPase axis unveils a potent host-directed strategy for eradicating intracellular pathogens and potentiating conventional antimicrobial therapy.

items.title: Characterisation of an unusual nicotinic acetylcholine receptor subtype preferentially sensitive to biogenic amines
items.author: Andrew K. Jones
items.content: Phylogenetic analysis, functional expression and computer-based simulations have highlighted how ligand-gated ion channels can evolve unusual pharmacological properties in a species-specific manner.

items.title: Planar lightsheet optical tweezer pLOT for 2D trapping and imaging of freely-moving live cells
items.author: Partha Pratim Mondal
items.content: Lightsheet based 2D optical tweezer (planar 2DLOT) allows trapping objects (living & non-living) in a 2D plane and simultaneous interrogation/imaging with diffraction-limited resolution.

items.title: GSK3 phosphorylates and activates trehalose-6-phosphate synthase to improve trehalose production and thermotolerance in Ganoderma lucidum
items.author: Huhui Chen
items.content: GSK3 promotes trehalose biosynthesis by phosphorylating TPS at Ser-529, thereby regulating the mycelial growth and response to high temperatures.

items.title: Insights on the intracellular trafficking of calcifying medium in a reef-building coral
items.author: Sylvie Tambutté
items.content: Observation of the intracellular trafficking of calcifying fluid in microcolonies of a reef-building coral sheds light on the cell biology of a calcifying organism.

items.title: miR-299a-5p is a mediator of fibrosis in diabetic kidney disease by regulating follistatin and cripto-1
items.author: Joan C. Krepinsky
items.content: miR-299a-5p is elevated in diabetic kidneys and in mesangial cells, promoting extracellular matrix buildup by reducing follistatin and cripto-1. This enhances activin A and TGFβ1 signaling. Its inhibition lessens fibrosis in diabetic kidneys.
